---
title: "Sprint 3: MVPVU Highlights Platform"
date: "2026-03-10"
categories: [Supabase, Vercel, Claude Code, Authentication]
---

## Overview

Families of young athletes often miss crucial game moments due to distance or busy schedules. Current solutions require downloading expensive apps or dealing with complex video hardware.

MVPVU solves this with a responsive web application that allows users to stream youth sports games, clip highlights instantly, and share them securely with family members — all without downloading an app.

## Key Capabilities

- Email/password authentication with Supabase
- Dashboard showing recorded games with scores and metadata
- Game watch view with timestamped scoring events
- Highlight creation tool with trim points, tags, and player tagging
- Row Level Security (RLS) ensuring data isolation per user
- Responsive design accessible on any device

## Live Product Demo

<div class="d-flex justify-content-center gap-3 my-4">
  <a class="btn btn-primary d-inline-flex align-items-center"
     href="https://mvpvu-app.vercel.app"
     target="_blank" rel="noopener"
     aria-label="Open live app">
    <i class="fa-solid fa-window-restore me-2" aria-hidden="true"></i>
    Open App in New Tab
  </a>
</div>

## Documentation

- [Product Specification](https://github.com/wyatt-44/mvpvu-website/blob/main/specs/product-spec.md)
- [CLAUDE.md Engineering Standards](https://github.com/wyatt-44/mvpvu-website/blob/main/CLAUDE.md)
- [Platform Strategy Justification](https://github.com/wyatt-44/mvpvu-website/blob/main/specs/platform-strategy.md)
- [Database Schema](https://github.com/wyatt-44/mvpvu-website/blob/main/specs/supabase-schema.sql)

## User Testing

After testing with 3 distinct user groups (a busy parent, a casual peer, and a sports power user), we achieved a **100% task completion rate**. All testers described the interface as "professional" and "clean."

Key findings:

- **Orientation Gap**: Users spent ~60 seconds scanning the page before finding specific tools
- **Save Highlight Glitch**: A technical error during highlight saving was flagged as critical
- **Terminology Barriers**: Sports jargon (e.g., "FT") confused non-player demographics
- **Empty States**: Blank video containers caused users to wonder if buttons were broken

[Full User Testing Synthesis](https://github.com/wyatt-44/mvpvu-website/blob/main/specs/user-testing-synthesis.md)

## Technical Details

Framework/Stack: HTML/CSS/JS, Supabase (PostgreSQL, Auth, RLS)

Deployment: Vercel (static site)

Tools Used:

- Claude Code
- Supabase Dashboard
- GitHub

## Learning Outcomes

1. **Supabase provides a full backend without server code** — auth, database, and RLS policies replaced what would normally require a Node.js backend
2. **Row Level Security is essential** for multi-user apps where data isolation matters
3. **User testing reveals blind spots** — the "Orientation Gap" and jargon issues were invisible to the builder but obvious to fresh eyes
